titleStmt.title: Letter from Arthur Megaw to Sir Robert Chalmers, 18 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Arthur Stanley Megaw

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Arthur Megaw to the under-secretary of Ireland.The letter concerns William Hayes Fisher’s, 1st Baron Downham (1853 – 1920), declaration in the House of Commons that different arrangements will be made for Ireland when appointing commissioners regarding the civil liberties of soldiers. Megaw is curious as to whether gentlemen having ‘special experience of similar work in this part of Ireland’ will be considered suitable for the position.Arthur Stanley Megaw (1872 – 1961) was a solicitor from Belfast. Megaw graduated from Queen’s University in 1894 and trained as a solicitor. In middle life he gave up the law to pursue his literary interests and established a reputation as an anthologist publishing under the name Arthur Stanley.The letter is addressed to the under-secretary, which at this time is Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938). Chalmers was sent to Ireland as under-secretary, replacing Sir Matthew Nathan who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary following the Easter Rising in 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary’s Office Registered papers, CSO RP/1916/8719/1

correspAction.placeName: 22 Ocean Buildings, Donegall Square East, Belfast, County Antrim, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
22, Ocean Buildings. Donegall Square East, Belfast. 18th May, 1916. Sir, Commissioners re Civil liabilities of Soldiers. Mr Hayes Fisher having stated in the House of Commons that 'different
arrangements' are being made for Ireland, will you kindly tell me by
whom the Irish appointments will be made, and whether it is likely that
gentlemen having experience of similar work in this part of
Ireland would be considered suitable? If I am not unduly trespassing
on your courtesy, may I ask you to be so kind as to send me a telegram
on receipt of this letter. With thanks in anticipation I am
Your obedient servant Arthur Megaw
The Under Secretary for Ireland
Dublin Castle
a.u.s.
We have no information on
this subject so far as I am aware
e.o.f.
19-V-16 Sir,
No information here as to the
arrangements referred to in your letter, the 20th
A.U.S
